steps.title: Map the logical relationship
steps.content: Sentence 1: voting members were impressed by the album and its contribution to the banda genre. Sentence 2: they awarded the group Best Banda Album. The award is the direct consequence of being impressed, so the transition must signal cause-to-effect.

steps.title: Test for a consequence transition
steps.content: Accordingly means 'as a result' / 'therefore' — it links a cause to its effect, which is exactly the relationship between admiration and the resulting award.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'In contrast,': signals opposition, but giving an award is consistent with (not opposed to) being impressed. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Meanwhile,': signals simultaneous unrelated events; it ignores the causal link between admiration and the award. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Nevertheless,': signals concession (despite X, Y); but the award happens because of, not in spite of, the members' admiration. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Accordingly,': signals consequence, correctly tying the award to the members' favorable impression. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
